For those who don't know💀

Grass is one of the most common and essential plants on Earth, yet many people rarely stop to consider what it actually is. Scientifically, grass belongs to the *Poaceae* family, also known as the *Gramineae* family. This family includes thousands of species, ranging from the tiny blades that make up your front lawn to the tall stalks of corn and bamboo. Grass is found on every continent, including Antarctica, and plays a vital role in ecosystems, agriculture, and even human civilization.
Grass is typically a *herbaceous plant**, meaning it doesn’t have woody stems. It grows in clumps or spreads through underground stems called **rhizomes* or above-ground stems called **stolons**. The most recognizable part of grass is the long, narrow **blade**, which is flat and usually green due to the presence of chlorophyll. These blades grow from the base, allowing the plant to keep growing even after being grazed or mowed, which is one reason grasses are so resilient.
Unlike many plants, grass flowers are small and not showy. They are usually pollinated by wind rather than insects. Over time, grasses can produce seeds, which help them spread and reproduce.
Grass is incredibly important to the environment. It provides *ground cover* that prevents soil erosion by holding the soil together with its root system. Grasslands, like prairies and savannas, are home to many species of animals and insects. These ecosystems are among the most biodiverse on Earth.
In addition, grass is a major part of the food chain. Herbivores like cows, sheep, and deer feed on it, and in turn, predators rely on those herbivores. Without grass, entire ecosystems would collapse.
Humans have used grass in various ways for thousands of years. Some grasses, like *wheat, rice, corn, barley,* and **oats**, are staple food crops and form the basis of diets around the world. These grasses are cultivated in large quantities and are essential for feeding the global population.
Other grasses, such as *bamboo**, are used as building materials, while **turf grasses* are planted in lawns, sports fields, and parks. These grasses not only provide beauty and recreational space but also help cool the air and reduce dust and noise.
Grass is more than just something we walk on or mow on weekends. It’s a complex and essential group of plants that supports ecosystems, feeds the world, and helps maintain the health of the planet. From agriculture to biodiversity, the importance of grass cannot be overstated. Understanding what grass is helps us appreciate its quiet but powerful role in nature and in our lives.

For those who don't know: Grass is one of the most common and important plants on Earth. It grows in fields, gardens, parks, and even along roadsides. Grass is green, soft, and short, and it covers the ground like a carpet. It needs sunlight, water, and soil to grow well.

Grass is very useful. Animals like cows, goats, and sheep eat grass as their main food. It also helps to prevent soil erosion by holding the soil together with its roots. In homes.

and public places, grass makes lawns look beautiful and provides a cool place to sit or play.

In addition, grass helps clean the air by taking in carbon dioxide and giving out oxygen. It also reduces noise and keeps the ground cool. Without grass, the environment would not be as healthy and pleasant.

In conclusion, grass may seem simple, but it plays a big role in nature and in our daily lives. We should take care of it and protect green spaces for a better world.

What is grass?

Grass is a type of flowering plant (family Poaceae) known for its thin, narrow leaves and hollow, jointed stems, which grow in large numbers to form a green carpet on the ground. These plants are vital for human life, providing food (like rice and wheat), and are essential for livestock. Ecologically, they help prevent soil erosion, absorb water, and purify air. They are also a source of recreation for sports and leisure.  
Botanical and ecological importance
Botanical structure:
Grasses are monocots with narrow, blade-like leaves and stems that are typically hollow between the joints. Their growing point is at the base of the leaf, not the tip, which allows them to be mown or grazed without being killed. 
Ecological functions:
Grasses are one of the most widespread plant families on Earth, playing a crucial role in ecosystems. They protect soil from erosion, absorb water, and help purify the air. 
Habitat:
Grasses have adapted to a wide variety of climates, from deserts to rainforests and grasslands, forming the foundation for ecosystems like savannas and prairies. 
Human uses and importance
Food:
The most economically important plant family, providing staple grains like rice, wheat, and corn, as well as forage for livestock. 
Materials:
Some grasses, like bamboo, are used for building materials, furniture, and utensils. 
Recreation:
Grass is cultivated for lawns, parks, and sports fields for recreation and leisure activities. 
Erosion control:
Certain types of grass are used as ground cover to prevent soil erosion. 
Other meanings of the word "grass"
Marijuana: In informal slang, "grass" is a common term for marijuana. 
Informant: In British slang, "to grass on" means to inform on someone, and a "grass" is a person who does so. 
Botanical group: The word "grass" is sometimes used to describe other similar-looking plants, such as sedges and rushes, which are not true grasses.

For those who don't know💀

Grass is one of the most common and essential plants on Earth, yet many people rarely stop to consider what it actually is. Scientifically, grass belongs to the Poaceae family, also known as the Gramineae family. This family includes thousands of species, ranging from the tiny blades that make up your front lawn to the tall stalks of corn and bamboo. Grass is found on every continent, including Antarctica, and plays a vital role in ecosystems, agriculture, and even human civilization.
Grass is typically a herbaceous plant**, meaning it doesn’t have woody stems. It grows in clumps or spreads through underground stems called **rhizomes or above-ground stems called **stolons**. The most recognizable part of grass is the long, narrow **blade**, which is flat and usually green due to the presence of chlorophyll. These blades grow from the base, allowing the plant to keep growing even after being grazed or mowed, which is one reason grasses are so resilient.
Unlike many plants, grass flowers are small and not showy. They are usually pollinated by wind rather than insects. Over time, grasses can produce seeds, which help them spread and reproduce.
Grass is incredibly important to the environment. It provides ground cover that prevents soil erosion by holding the soil together with its root system. Grasslands, like prairies and savannas, are home to many species of animals and insects. These ecosystems are among the most biodiverse on Earth.
In addition, grass is a major part of the food chain. Herbivores like cows, sheep, and deer feed on it, and in turn, predators rely on those herbivores. Without grass, entire ecosystems would collapse.
Humans have used grass in various ways for thousands of years. Some grasses, like wheat, rice, corn, barley, and **oats**, are staple food crops and form the basis of diets around the world. These grasses are cultivated in large quantities and are essential for feeding the global population.
Other grasses, such as bamboo**, are used as building materials, while **turf grasses are planted in lawns, sports fields, and parks. These grasses not only provide beauty and recreational space but also help cool the air and reduce dust and noise.
Grass is more than just something we walk on or mow on weekends. It’s a complex and essential group of plants that supports ecosystems, feeds the world, and helps maintain the health of the planet. From agriculture to biodiversity, the importance of grass cannot be overstated. Understanding what grass is helps us appreciate its quiet but powerful role in nature and in our lives.

✨ Storage Upgrade to Save Grow A Garden ✨
Hi Kreek! By watching your video + checking players’ opinions, I found real ways that can revive GAG. Here is one of the biggest fixes:

Right now everything goes into the bag icon — seeds, crops, pets, tools… It gets confusing and boring to manage. We need storage that feels like a REAL farm:

✅ Barn — stores all crops/fruits → organized & easy to sell
✅ Seed Cabinet — clean seed storage with sorting & quick select
✅ Stable — keeps pets in one place, preview + easy trading
✅ Gear Locker — holds tools like shovels, ploughs, sprinklers

📌 Why this helps:
• Fixes inventory clutter players always complain about
• Makes gardens feel alive, not just icons in a menu
• Encourages collecting more stuff because storage grows
• Better trading — no more asking “show pets???” every time
• Unlock & upgrade using shekels → stronger economy

This is a small change with BIG impact on fun & player growth.
Grow a Garden can be the GOAT again! 🌱

📌 NOTE — Important for New Players!
All new storage buildings (Barn, Seed Cabinet, Stable, Gear Locker) will be already unlocked from the start — because new players don’t have shekels and they need a place to store carrots and basic items immediately. These buildings will be inside the garden by default and players can sell crops directly from the Barn. Upgrading storage will cost shekels later, but beginners won’t be forced to grind just to store things. This keeps gameplay smooth and fair for EVERYONE — new and old players.

Thank you so much for bringing the first issue of the video up KreekCraft, it was about time it got coverage. My husband and I both live in a third-world country. Months ago my husband got an issue which changed his regional pricing to be a lot higher, this persists even today. We thought it might've been just a one-off issue since he isn't in the same network all the time unlike me. Until I recently got the same issue after he bought me premium. Not only did regional prices stop applying for me. I also am being charged more for the purchase of more items. We have both contacted Roblox support for this and sent numerous amounts of tickets, all of the replies we got were either AI or told us that it was an issue on our end and that there was nothing they could do about it. I really hope more people talk about this more often as it is a really big deal for those of us who live in countries that already struggle a lot economically. I doubt Roblox will do anything about this anytime soon, but the more coverage it gets, the more likely our chances will be.

As an Italian (who hates Italian brainrot but that's besides the point), allow me to explain more precisely:
The bad stuff isn't from a song. Rather, it's from the original uncut audio of the memes.
As most people know, Tralalero Tralala and Bombardiro Crocodilo originate from italian videos with an image of the character accompanied by audio where in the beginning of the video they state their name, which is the popular audio that you hear on Tiktok and that every developer is putting in their games. 
Now, what most people don't know is that the audio continues. These memes were made as dark humor and say some very controversial things. The Tralalero Tralala video uses blasphemy against God and Allah (in Italian there's basically a highest tier of swear words that are worse than any other swear and are very blasphemous, which Kreek probably should have censored by the way). 
While in the case of the Bombardiro Crocodilo video, it talks about bombing children in Palestine.
Obviously, people don't like kids interacting with this kind of content, hence this whole situation.
